heading: Lifespan respite care grants and cooperative agreements
label: 300ii–1 Lifespan respite care grants and cooperative agreements

children.text: to expand and enhance respite care services to family caregivers;
to improve the statewide dissemination and coordination of respite care; and
to provide, supplement, or improve access and quality of respite care services to family caregivers, thereby reducing family caregiver strain.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: b Authorization
children.text: Subject to subsection (e), the Secretary is authorized to award grants or cooperative agreements for the purposes described in subsection (a) to eligible State agencies for which an application is submitted pursuant to subsection (d).

children.heading: Federal lifespan approach
children.label: c Federal lifespan approach
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary shall work in cooperation with the National Family Caregiver Support Program of the Administration on Aging and other respite care programs within the Department of Health and Human Services to ensure coordination of respite care services for family caregivers of children and adults with special needs.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: 1 Submission
children.text: Each Governor desiring the eligible State agency of his or her State to receive a grant or cooperative agreement under this section shall submit an application on behalf of such agency to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary shall require.

children.label: i
children.text: ability to work with other State and community-based agencies;

children.label: ii
children.text: understanding of respite care and family caregiver issues across all age groups, disabilities, and chronic conditions; and

children.label: iii
children.text: capacity to ensure meaningful involvement of family members, family caregivers, and care recipients;

children.label: i
children.text: the population of family caregivers;

children.label: ii
children.text: the extent and nature of the respite care needs of that population;

children.label: iii
children.text: existing respite care services for that population, including numbers of family caregivers being served and extent of unmet need;

children.label: iv
children.text: existing methods or systems to coordinate respite care information and services to the population at the State and local level and extent of unmet need;

children.label: v
children.text: how respite care information dissemination and coordination, respite care services, respite care worker and volunteer recruitment and training programs, or training programs for family caregivers that assist such family caregivers in making informed decisions about respite care services will be provided using grant or cooperative agreement funds;

children.label: vi
children.text: a plan for administration, collaboration, and coordination of the proposed respite care activities with other related services or programs offered by public or private, nonprofit entities, including area agencies on aging;

children.label: vii
children.text: how the population, including family caregivers, care recipients, and relevant public or private agencies, will participate in the planning and implementation of the proposed respite care activities;

children.label: viii
children.text: how the proposed respite care activities will make use, to the maximum extent feasible, of other Federal, State, and local funds, programs, contributions, other forms of reimbursements, personnel, and facilities;

children.label: ix
children.text: respite care services available to family caregivers in the eligible State agency’s State or locality, including unmet needs and how the eligible State agency’s plan for use of funds will improve the coordination and distribution of respite care services for family caregivers of children and adults with special needs;

children.label: x
children.text: the criteria used to identify family caregivers eligible for respite care services;

children.label: xi
children.text: how the quality and safety of any respite care services provided will be monitored, including methods to ensure that respite care workers and volunteers are appropriately screened and possess the necessary skills to care for the needs of the care recipient in the absence of the family caregiver; and

children.label: xii
children.text: the results expected from proposed respite care activities and the procedures to be used for evaluating those results;

children.label: C
children.text: assurances that, where appropriate, the eligible State agency will have a system for maintaining the confidentiality of care recipient and family caregiver records; and

children.label: i
children.text: the eligible State agency; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a public or private nonprofit statewide respite coalition or organization.
give priority to eligible State agencies that the Secretary determines show the greatest likelihood of implementing or enhancing lifespan respite care statewide; and

give consideration to eligible State agencies that are building or enhancing the capacity of their long-term care systems to respond to the comprehensive needs, including respite care needs, of their residents.

children.heading: Use of grant or cooperative agreement funds
children.label: f Use of grant or cooperative agreement funds
i
children.text: to develop or enhance lifespan respite care at the State and local levels;

children.label: ii
children.text: to provide respite care services for family caregivers caring for children or adults;

children.label: iii
children.text: to train and recruit respite care workers and volunteers;

children.label: iv
children.text: to provide information to caregivers about available respite and support services; and

children.label: v
children.text: to assist caregivers in gaining access to such services.

children.label: i
children.text: training programs for family caregivers to assist such family caregivers in making informed decisions about respite care services;

children.label: ii
children.text: other services essential to the provision of respite care as the Secretary may specify; or

children.label: iii
children.text: training and education for new caregivers.

children.heading: Subcontracts
children.label: 2 Subcontracts
children.text: Each eligible State agency awarded a grant or cooperative agreement under this section may carry out the activities described in paragraph (1) directly or by grant to, or contract with, public or private entities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to the costs of the activities to be carried out under paragraph (1), a condition for the receipt of a grant or cooperative agreement under this section is that the eligible State agency agrees to make available (directly or through donations from public or private entities) non-Federal contributions toward such costs in an amount that is not less than 25 percent of such costs.

children.heading: Determination of amount contributed
children.label: B Determination of amount contributed
children.text: Non-Federal contributions required by subparagraph (A) may be in cash or in kind, fairly evaluated, including plant, equipment, or services. Amounts provided by the Federal Government, or services assisted or subsidized to any significant extent by the Federal Government, may not be included in determining the amount of such non-Federal contributions.

children.heading: Term of grants or cooperative agreements
children.label: g Term of grants or cooperative agreements

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants or cooperative agreements under this section for terms that do not exceed 5 years.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: 2 Renewal
children.text: The Secretary may renew a grant or cooperative agreement under this section at the end of the term of the grant or cooperative agreement determined under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Maintenance of effort
children.label: h Maintenance of effort
children.text: Funds made available under this section shall be used to supplement and not supplant other Federal, State, and local funds available for respite care services.
